title: Document Validate dependency references at bead creation and scheduler startup

handoff_summary.completed: Added concise docstrings in RepositoryStorage for dependency validation, save-time enforcement, and scheduler-ready filtering, plus test docstrings describing create-time rejection, save-time rejection, and scheduler handling of corrupt dependency references.
handoff_summary.remaining: No further documentation work remains within this bead's allowed file scope.
handoff_summary.risks: No tests were run per documentation-agent guardrails. Broader user-facing docs were not updated because this bead was restricted to the two scoped files.
handoff_summary.conflict_risks: RepositoryStorage.save_bead() documentation now explicitly reflects strict dependency existence checks, and ready_beads() documentation now calls out that corrupt on-disk dependency references are skipped with a warning record. Downstream agents should still expect ValueError for invalid dependency lists and scheduler-side resilience for legacy/corrupt bead files.

conflict_risks: RepositoryStorage.save_bead() documentation now explicitly reflects strict dependency existence checks, and ready_beads() documentation now calls out that corrupt on-disk dependency references are skipped with a warning record. Downstream agents should still expect ValueError for invalid dependency lists and scheduler-side resilience for legacy/corrupt bead files.

execution_history.summary: Deferred due to file-scope conflict with active bead B-cc3d06ce-test
Documented inline dependency-validation and runtime-resilience behavior in the scoped storage and test files.

guardrails.template_text:
# Documentation Guardrails

Primary responsibility: Update documentation and examples that explain the assigned behavior without changing runtime feature behavior.

Allowed actions:
- Edit docs, examples, and explanatory text tied to the assigned bead.
- Focus only on files listed in the bead's touched_files and changed_files fields. Do not read or modify unrelated documentation.
- Align documentation with existing code and validated behavior.
- Identify when implementation or tests must land first and block with a handoff.

Disallowed actions:
- Change runtime behavior, production code paths, or feature logic.
- Invent undocumented behavior that is not present in the codebase.
- Approve code quality or test completeness as a substitute for review.
- Do not run the test suite. Testing is the tester agent's responsibility.

Efficiency constraints:
- Do not read the full codebase for context. Focus on the changed files and their immediate surroundings.
- Keep documentation updates concise and proportional to the change.

Expected outputs:
- Return JSON with `outcome` set to `completed` (documentation beads always complete; use `verdict` for pass/fail) and `summary` as a one-line description of what was documented.
- Return JSON with structured verdict fields for every run: `verdict`, `findings_count`, and `requires_followup`.
- Use `verdict=approved`, `findings_count=0`, and `requires_followup=false` when documentation is complete with no gaps.
- Use `verdict=needs_changes`, set `findings_count` to the number of unresolved documentation gaps, set `requires_followup=true`, and include `block_reason` when implementation must land first or documentation cannot be completed.
- Keep `completed`, `remaining`, and `risks` as concise narrative context only; they do not override the structured verdict fields.
- Accurate updated docs and clear next-agent recommendations when code changes are required first.

worker_prompt_context.title: Document Validate dependency references at bead creation and scheduler startup
worker_prompt_context.conflict_risks: RepositoryStorage.save_bead() is now stricter and will reject missing dependency IDs across all callers. Downstream agents touching bead persistence should expect ValueError on invalid dependency lists. Scheduler.py was not changed; the runtime defense lives in storage.ready_beads().

last_agent_result.summary: Documented inline dependency-validation and runtime-resilience behavior in the scoped storage and test files.
